question.title: Will Anthropic be a designated supply chain risk on May 1, 2026?
question.description:
Under [10 U.S.C. § 3252](https://www.law.cornell.edu/uscode/text/10/3252), the U.S. Secretary of Defense, of the Army, of the Navy, or of the Air Force may exclude certain "sources" from competing for government contracts (or subcontracts) for so-called [national security systems](https://www.law.cornell.edu/uscode/text/44/3552#b_6) in order to reduce "supply chain risk." Supply chain risk is defined in the statute to mean the risk that an adversary may sabotage or subvert the relevant national security system.

Separately, under [41 U.S.C § 4713](https://www.law.cornell.edu/uscode/text/41/4713), the head of an executive agency may exclude a "source" from competing for government contacts for certain information technology or telecommunications systems, in order to reduce supply chain risk. Similarly here, supply chain risk is defined to mean the risk that a person may sabotage or manipulate the system(s) in question. By law, a party excluded under this statute [may petition for judicial review](https://www.law.cornell.edu/uscode/text/41/1327). In September 2025, [Acronis AG, a Swedish cybersecurity and data protection company](https://natlawreview.com/article/order-first-fascsa-order-has-been-issued-odni), became the first entity known to be excluded under this law.

[Anthropic](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Anthropic) is an American AI company, founded in 2021 by former employees of OpenAI who left due to ["differences" over the latter's "direction."](https://venturebeat.com/ai/as-anthropic-seeks-billions-to-take-on-openai-industrial-capture-is-nigh-or-is-it) Anthropic is structured as a [public benefit corporation](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Benefit_corporation) and states that it is dedicated to building [safe](https://www.anthropic.com/company) AI systems. In 2024, Anthropic (and other AI companies, including OpenAI) [changed their policies](https://techcrunch.com/2025/01/19/the-pentagon-says-ai-is-speeding-up-its-kill-chain/) to allow U.S. intelligence and defense agencies use their software, and in January 2026, Anthropic's "Claude" tool [was used](https://www.wsj.com/politics/national-security/pentagon-used-anthropics-claude-in-maduro-venezuela-raid-583aff17) in the United States' raid and capture of Venezuelan President Nicolás Maduro.

In late February 2026, Anthropic was [in talks with the Department of Defense](https://www.nytimes.com/2026/03/01/technology/anthropic-defense-dept-openai-talks.html) over a proposed contract. Although the two sides were reportedly close to a deal, there was unresolved disagreement around the government's demand for "unfettered use" of Anthropic's software, which Anthropic opposed. (In [Anthropic's view](https://www.anthropic.com/news/statement-department-of-war), its software should not be used for "mass domestic surveillance" or "fully autonomous weapons.") Then, on February 27, 2026, President Donald Trump [posted online](https://truthsocial.com/@realDonaldTrump/posts/116144552969293195) that the government would no longer use Anthropic's software:

> The Leftwing nut jobs at Anthropic have made a DISASTROUS MISTAKE trying to STRONG-ARM the Department of War, and force them to obey their Terms of Service instead of our Constitution. …
>  
> Therefore, I am directing EVERY Federal Agency in the United States Government to IMMEDIATELY CEASE all use of Anthropic’s technology. We don’t need it, we don’t want it, and will not do business with them again! There will be a Six Month phase out period for Agencies like the Department of War who are using Anthropic’s products, at various levels.

An hour and a half later, Secretary Hegseth [declared](https://x.com/SecWar/status/2027507717469049070) that Anthropic was a supply chain risk:

> In conjunction with the President's directive for the Federal Government to cease all use of Anthropic's technology, I am directing the Department of War to designate Anthropic a Supply-Chain Risk to National Security. Effective immediately, no contractor, supplier, or partner that does business with the United States military may conduct any commercial activity with Anthropic. Anthropic will continue to provide the Department of War its services for a period of no more than six months to allow for a seamless transition to a better and more patriotic service.

Legal observers have [opined](https://www.lawfaremedia.org/article/pentagon%27s-anthropic-designation-won%27t-survive-first-contact-with-legal-system) that Hegseth's action was [likely unlawful](https://www.justsecurity.org/132851/anthropic-supply-chain-risk-designation/) and [unlikely to stand up in court](https://www.lawfaremedia.org/article/pentagon%27s-anthropic-designation-won%27t-survive-first-contact-with-legal-system). And indeed Anthropic has vowed to challenge the designation, though as of March 3, 2026, no lawsuit is known to have been filed.

`{"format":"metac_reveal_and_close_in_period","info":{"post_id":42491,"question_id":42289}}`
question.resolution_criteria: This question will resolve as Yes if, on May 1, 2026, Anthropic has been excluded from obtaining government contracts under either 10 U.S.C. § 3252 or 41 U.S.C. § 4713 and that exclusion is currently in effect.
question.fine_print:
An exclusion that is enjoined or stayed by a court or withdrawn by the Trump administration as of May 1, 2026, will not cause this question to resolve as Yes.

The six-month wind-down period announced by Donald Trump and Pete Hegseth will not cause this question to resolve as No as long as Anthropic is excluded from obtaining new contracts.

***
This question's information (resolution criteria, fine print, background info, etc) is synced with an [original identical question](https://www.metaculus.com/questions/42491) which opened on 2026-03-04 17:00:00. This question will resolve based on the resolution criteria and fine print contained above. However, if this question would resolve differently than the original question, then this question will be annulled. Additionally, if the original question's resolution could have been known before this question opened, then this question will be annulled.
question.short_title: Will Anthropic be a designated supply chain risk on May 1, 2026?
